title: Fat rascals
subtitle: Yorkshire spiced fruit tea cakes
excerpt: Fat rascals are a Yorkshire version of a glorified rock cake: a thick, slightly sweet, spiced dough with currants and mixed peel, traditionally decorated with glacé cherries and blanched almonds pressed into the top before baking. They are richer and larger than a standard rock cake.
sourceNotes: Fat rascals are a Yorkshire regional bakery item, documented as a speciality of Bettys café in Harrogate from the 1980s, though the underlying fruit and spice cake tradition is much older. The recipe form (a rich fruit drop scone with cherry-and-almond face decoration) has been reproduced in British domestic cookbooks and regional recipes since the late twentieth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled. Warm at 180°C for 8 minutes from frozen.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best the day they are baked. Keep for 2 days in an airtight tin.

glossaryTerms.definition: The traditional decoration on a fat rascal: two glacé cherries pressed in for eyes and a row of blanched almonds arranged as teeth or a smile across the front. Applied before baking so the decoration bonds to the surface. The face is the identifying mark of the Bettys version that made the fat rascal famous beyond Yorkshire.

content.text: (two cherry eyes and almond teeth) is optional but traditional. The decoration is pressed in before baking so it sets into the surface. Chill the shaped rascals briefly before baking to help them hold their thick, rounded shape.

Rub {{unsalted-butter}} into {{self-raising-flour}} until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Stir in {{caster-sugar}}, {{mixed-spice}}, {{currants}}, {{mixed-peel}}, and {{lemon}} zest. Add the beaten {{eggs}} and {{double-cream}} and mix to a firm dough. Divide into 8 portions. Shape each into a thick, slightly flattened round about 7 cm across. Place on lined baking trays and press in the cherry and almond decoration if using. Chill for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 190°C (170°C fan).
Bake for 18–20 minutes until golden and set. The rascals should be firm when pressed gently at the edge. Cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before eating.
